source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
artist: Unknown photographer (LoC Miscellaneous Items in High Demand)
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Scene near Kodiak, Alaska
description: 1916 photograph of a sheltered inlet near Kodiak, Alaska, showing the forested headlands, a small wooden structure on shore, and calm protected waters — the kind of landscape that surrounds the town on all sides. Kodiak Island is the second-largest island in the United States after the Big Island of Hawaii.
alt: 1916 black-and-white photograph of a sheltered inlet near Kodiak, Alaska with forested headlands and calm protected waters
